revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23987730427|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:伏見桃山陵]][[ko:후시미모모야마 능]][[th:สุสานหลวงฟูชิมิโมโมยามะ]][[vi:Lăng Fushimi Momoyama]][[zh:伏見桃山陵]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum |image = [[File:Fushimi momoyaja jyou 13.jpg|250px]] |location = {{flag|Kyoto Prefecture}}
[[File:Flag_of_Kyoto_City.svg|border|25px]] [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Fushimi Ward|Fushimi Ward]], [[Momoyama Hills|Momoyama Town]] Kojōyama |coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|13.8|N|135|46|52.3|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum (Meiji Emperor's Tomb)}} |type = [[Upper circular, lower square mound]] |area = |built = {{ill|1912|en|1912|qid=Q2077|lt=1912|1=1912}} (Taishō Year 1) |Burial Facility = |Interred Persons = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} |burial goods = |陵墓 = Managed by the Imperial Household Agency as "Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum" |Special Notes = }} [[File:Fushimimomoyama-higashi-misasagi.jpg|thumb|180px|Fushimi Momoyama Eastern Mausoleum]] '''Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum''' (伏見桃山陵, Fushimi Momoyama no Misazaki or Fushimi Momoyama Ryō) is the imperial mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} located on the [[Momoyama Hills]] in [[:en:Fushimi Ward|Fushimi Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as the "Momoyama Mausoleum" (桃山御陵, Momoyama Goryō). == Overview == On [[July 30, 1912]], Emperor Meiji passed away at the [[Meiji Palace]] in Tokyo. The [[:en:State Funeral|State Funeral]] was held at the [[Aoyama Parade Ground]] (now the [[:en:Meiji Jingu Gaien|Meiji Jingu Gaien]]) on [[September 13, 1912]] (Taishō Year 1), followed by burial on {{ill|September 14|en|September 14|qid=Q2847|lt=September 14|1=September 14}}. The mausoleum site, {{ill|Momoyama|en|Momoyama|qid=Q98383897|lt=Momoyama|1=Momoyama}}, occupies the [[:en:honmaru|honmaru]] (main keep) ruins of {{ill|Fushimi Castle|en|Fushimi Castle|qid=Q1196273|lt=Fushimi Castle|1=Fushimi Castle}} built by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. Emperor Meiji's wish dictated his burial location in Kyoto. Adjacent to the east is the '''Fushimi Momoyama Eastern Mausoleum''' (伏見桃山東陵, Fushimi Momoyama no Higashi no Misazaki), the resting place of Empress [[Shōken]]. The mausoleum is also near the [[Kashiwabara Mausoleum]] of [[Emperor Kanmu]]. The surrounding area is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Momoyama Mausoleum Grounds," which oversees imperial tombs in the southwestern part of Kyoto City, the old Sanyōdō, and Saikaidō regions. The mound takes the type of an [[Upper circular, lower square mound|ancient kofun-style mound]], with a square lower base approximately 60 meters on each side and a circular upper mound 6.3 meters in height, surfaced with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones). Inside, a square burial pit reinforced with concrete houses a wooden coffin encased in lime and capped with a stone lid. The tomb's design was inspired by the [[Tomb of Emperor Tenji]] in Yamashina. This choice reflects a rejection of larger, grander designs such as the [[Tomb of Emperor Ōjin]] or [[:en:Tomb of Emperor Nintoku|Tomb of Emperor Nintoku]], aligning with Emperor Meiji's frugality and honoring Emperor Tenji's role as a retypeer during the [[Taika Retypes]].'検証 天皇陵' by Sotoike Noboru (2016) pp.298-299 ISBN 978-4-634-15102-4 Unique to this site, haniwa (terracotta figures) were recreated for the first time in over a millennium, based on Emperor Kanmu's custom of burying effigies for protective purposes. Four figures—two with bows and two with spears—were placed at the mound's corners. These were designed by historians such as [[Miyake Yonekichi]] and [[Wada Chikichi]] and crafted by sculptor Yoshida Hakurei.{{Cite web|url=https://www.modernart.museum.ibk.ed.jp/manage/contents/upload/63da1faae4d39.pdf|title=Resurrected Haniwa and Emerging Identities|access-date=2017-07-17|publisher=Hanai Hisanori}} After Emperor [[Kōmei]]'s burial, cremation was abandoned in favor of a return to ancient practices before Emperor [[:en:Tenmu|Tenmu]].'山陵' by Ueno Takejirō (1929, expanded 1925) Imperial tombs were traditionally built west of the Kansai region. However, following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the relocation of the capital to Tokyo, subsequent emperors were interred in the {{ill|Musashi Imperial Graveyard|en|Musashi Imperial Graveyard|qid=Q6940153|lt=Musashi Imperial Graveyard|1=Musashi Imperial Graveyard}} in [[:en:Hachioji|Hachioji]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. These include {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} (Tama Mausoleum) and [[:en:Emperor Shōwa|Emperor Shōwa]] (Musashino Mausoleum). == Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Nara Line|Nara Line]] - {{ill|Momoyama Station|en|Momoyama Station|qid=Q1048827|lt=Momoyama Station|1=Momoyama Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|qid=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|en|Keihan Uji Line|qid=Q1702750|lt=Keihan Uji Line|1=Keihan Uji Line}} - [[Momoyama Minami-guchi Station]] * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|qid=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Keihan Main Line|en|Keihan Main Line|qid=Q2598752|lt=Keihan Main Line|1=Keihan Main Line}} - [[:en:Fushimi Momoyama Station|Fushimi Momoyama Station]] * {{ill|Kintetsu|en|Kintetsu|qid=Q27794748|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu}} {{ill|Kyoto Line|en|Kyoto Line|qid=Q1181487|lt=Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line}} - {{ill|Momoyamagoryō-mae Station|en|Momoyamagoryō-mae Station|qid=Q6897485|lt=Momoyamagoryō-mae Station|1=Momoyamagoryō-mae Station}} {{wide image|伏見桃山陵 - パノラマ写真.jpg|1000px|Panoramic view of Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum}} == Trivia == The 230 steps at Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum correspond to [[October 30, 1890]] (Meiji 23), the date of the [[Imperial Rescript on Education]]. The number of steps represents the multiplication of the year (23) and the month (10). Additionally, the seven steps within the mausoleum grounds complete the calculation to the day (30). == Incidents == * [[May 4, 2024]] - The {{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|qid=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya|1=Chōzuya}} at Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum was vandalized with spray paint. A local high school student was later charged with {{ill|property damage|en|property damage|qid=Q554774|lt=property damage|1=Property damage}}.{{Cite web|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20240528-OYT1T50051/|title=Teen Vandalizes Temizuya at Emperor Meiji's Mausoleum|publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun ONLINE|date=2024-05-28|access-date=2024-06-14}} == Related Sites == * [[Sōkyō Plan]] * [[Nogi Shrine (Kyoto)]] - Located at the foot of Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mausoleum of Emperor Meiji}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/122/index.html Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://map.yahoo.co.jp/place?lat=34.93688106878482&lon=135.781200580619&zoom=16&maptype=basic map of Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum/YAHOO! maps] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|伏見桃山陵|version=103900316|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[th:%E0%B8%AA%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%AA%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%A7%E0%B8%87%E0%B8%9F%E0%B8%B9%E0%B8%8A%E0%B8%B4%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B4%E0%B9%82%E0%B8%A1%E0%B9%82%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B0]] [[vi:L%C4%83ng Fushimi Momoyama]] [[ko:%ED%9B%84%EC%8B%9C%EB%AF%B8%EB%AA%A8%EB%AA%A8%EC%95%BC%EB%A7%88 %EB%8A%A5]] [[zh:%E4%BC%8F%E8%A6%8B%E6%A1%83%E5%B1%B1%E9%99%B5]] [[ja:伏見桃山陵]] {{Commons category|Mausoleum of Emperor Meiji}} [[ko:후시미모모야마 능]] [[th:สุสานหลวงฟูชิมิโมโมยามะ]] [[vi:Lăng Fushimi Momoyama]] [[zh:伏見桃山陵]] {{Commons category|Mausoleum of Emperor Meiji}} [[ko:후시미모모야마 능]] [[th:สุสานหลวงฟูชิมิโมโมยามะ]] [[vi:Lăng Fushimi Momoyama]] [[zh:伏見桃山陵]] {{wikidata link|Q10885331}} [[Category:Emperor Meiji]] [[Category:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Momoyama]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
